name: NSubstitute
description: Friendly .NET substitute/mock library with simple syntax (Substitute.ForT()) and no record/replay phases.

seo.title: NSubstitute — Test Automation / Device Cloud
seo.meta_description: NSubstitute: Friendly .NET substitute/mock library with simple syntax (Substitute.ForT()) and no record/replay phases.

when_to_use: No record/replay phases for cleaner .NET test code
Received() assertions for method call verification
Auto-properties and default return values for fakes

cons: Cannot mock static or sealed classes
Less powerful than Moq for complex scenarios
review.verdict: The friendliest .NET mocking library if you want readable tests without Moq's lambda-heavy syntax.
review.best_for: .NET developers who value readable mock syntax over maximum flexibility
review.not_for: Teams needing to mock static methods or sealed third-party classes
